title: 'Civil War' Fends Off 'Abigail' With $11 Million at Box Office
text: Civil War fended off the challenge from Abigail at the box office, earning $11 million in its second weekend while Abigail opened with $10.2 million. The close competition between these two R-rated films underscored the intensity of the marketplace for adult-oriented content during the Easter weekend period. Civil War's relatively small 57% drop in its second weekend demonstrated strong audience retention, suggesting that Alex Garland's film had built solid word-of-mouth and appeal. Abigail's opening weekend of $10.2 million domestically, combined with $5.2 million internationally for a $15.2 million global opening, came in slightly below the $12-15 million industry projections. Universal's vampire horror film received positive critical reviews (82% on Rotten Tomatoes) but achieved only a B CinemaScore from audiences, indicating mixed reception. The film's $28 million budget positioned it to potentially break even over its full theatrical run, though industry observers noted that the below-forecast opening raised questions about the strength of the vampire genre and horror in general at the 2024 box office.
